title: WH_ERROR_GENERAL misused as sentinel for unrelated errors
description: Two sites use WolfHsmError::Wh { code: -2000 } (WH_ERROR_GENERAL) to signal conditions that are not WH_ERROR_GENERAL: (1) client.rs register_cryptocb line 59: 'already registered' maps to -2000. (2) key.rs key_cache line 40: 'data too large for u16' maps to -2000. In both cases the error display will say 'wolfHSM error -2000' which looks like a server error, not a local precondition failure. Suggested rewrite: add a WolfHsmError::Client { msg: &'static str } variant (or reuse WolfHsmError::Ffi with code: -1 and a descriptive func string, which is already done in other precondition checks in the same file) for locally-detected errors that have no WH error code. Using Ffi { code: -1, func: "register_cryptocb: already registered" } is self-consistent with the existing pattern in echo() and nvm_write().
